body.title: [sprint] Repo-map dependency-graph (PageRank) ranking — needs an import graph first
body.intention: I intend that the signature map ranks files the way aider does — by dependency centrality — so the most-depended-on structure surfaces first, because keyword relevance alone misses files that matter structurally but don't match the ticket's words.
body.description:
**Problem:** T-coding-repo-map-orientation shipped a RELEVANCE-ranked signature map (files ranked by aggregate keyword score). aider ranks by a graph algorithm over a dependency graph (files=nodes, import/reference edges), so a heavily-depended-on module surfaces even when its name doesn't match the ticket. Our clan.code_index has NO edge data (columns: path, symbol, kind, summary, embedding), so graph-rank could not ship in that ticket.
**Proposed shape:** build an import/reference graph for the repo (parse imports + symbol references — tree-sitter or the existing indexer), persist edges (new table or extend code_index), and rank the signature map's files by a centrality score (PageRank-style) blended with keyword/embedding relevance. Feed the re-ranked map into build_signature_map's file ordering.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/scraps/orientation_classifier.py (build_signature_map ranking), a new graph/edge builder + store, the indexer that populates code_index.
**Design rules:** prefer extending the existing indexer/symbols table over a parallel one (homogeneity); NO SQLITE (Postgres edges); log the crossing; proof-on-close.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the edge graph + centrality rank + blending into build_signature_map. OUT — the signature-map rendering itself (shipped) and embedding-similarity ranking (separate option).
**Test plan:** unit test — a file with LOW keyword match but HIGH in-degree (many importers) ranks above a high-keyword low-centrality file once graph-rank is blended in. Behavioral red: relevance-only ranking puts the high-keyword file first.
**Completion criteria:** build_signature_map file ordering incorporates a dependency-centrality score AND a test proves a high-centrality low-keyword file outranks a low-centrality high-keyword file.
**Rollback:** the centrality blend is a flag; disabling restores pure relevance ranking.

**Why deferred here:** the edge data must be built first; splitting it keeps T-coding-repo-map-orientation shippable and provable now (advisor 2026-07-04).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
